evals.prompt: I have a public PDF URL and I need Markdown quickly with no API token setup. Use MinerU lightweight parsing with page range 1-5 and table extraction enabled, then tell me how to poll and get the markdown URL.
evals.expected_output: Chooses URL mode, uses /api/v1/agent/parse/url with appropriate JSON fields, explains asynchronous polling via /api/v1/agent/parse/{task_id}, and returns a concise result format including task_id/state/markdown_url.

evals.prompt: I only have a local file ./slides.pptx. Use MinerU lightweight file mode and save the converted Markdown next to the source file as ./slides.md.
evals.expected_output: Chooses file mode, calls /api/v1/agent/parse/file with file_name, performs PUT upload to file_url, polls /api/v1/agent/parse/{task_id} until done, fetches markdown_url, and saves the Markdown beside the source file with the same basename and .md extension.

evals.prompt: My document is 42 pages and failed with err_code -30003 on the lightweight API. What should I do next with the smallest change to my workflow?
evals.expected_output: Correctly interprets -30003 as page limit overflow for lightweight API and recommends page_range chunking into 20-page-or-smaller requests, then merging Markdown chunks in page order. It should suggest MinerU standard API only when file size exceeds 10MB or one server-side job is required.

evals.prompt: MinerU file mode returns a task_id, but the PUT to file_url fails with Aliyun OSS HTTP 403 SignatureDoesNotMatch. Debug the likely cause and tell me the smallest code change.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the upload step as the failure point, explains that automatic headers such as Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded can invalidate the signed URL, and recommends uploading with a minimal PUT that preserves the signed URL exactly and sends only Content-Length unless explicitly required.

evals.prompt: Convert every PDF under a course folder into Markdown under ./md. Some files have the same basename and the slides are over 20 pages.
evals.expected_output: Uses client-side batching because MinerU has no batch endpoint, preserves the source folder layout under ./md to avoid collisions, converts normal PDFs directly, chunks long PDFs with page_range values of 20 pages or fewer, merges chunks in page order, and writes a manifest with per-file status.
